sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)

prose:
Fukui reactivity indices computed for phenol (13 atoms: O, 6×C, 6×H) at the DFT level using tier 'standard' (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, density-fit) in PySCF. Gas phase (solvent: null). Charge 0, multiplicity 1 for the neutral molecule; cation charge +1 mult 2; anion charge -1 mult 2. Partial charges via Mulliken scheme. Geometry taken directly from the user-supplied phenol.xyz without re-optimization at this level. All three charge states converged; both Fukui sum rules satisfied (sum(f+)=1.0000, sum(f-)=1.0000). Integrity: trustworthy=true.

Headline result: most electrophilic site is atom 3 (C, ortho ring carbon) with f+ = 2.1028319; most nucleophilic site is atom 1 (O) with f- = 0.16201477. Dual descriptor (f+−f−) is largest at atom 3 (2.03186), confirming ortho/para carbon positions as primary electrophilic centers and the oxygen as the dominant nucleophilic donor — consistent with the known ortho/para-directing effect of the hydroxyl group in electrophilic aromatic substitution.
